key_takeaways: GE Vernova delivered record Q4 2024 orders of $13.2B (+22% YoY) and full-year orders of $44B, growing total backlog to $119B with equipment backlog margin up ~5 points, underpinning strong multi-year revenue and margin visibility.

Power and Electrification segments are the primary growth engines: gas turbine demand is doubling with capacity expanding to 70-80 units/year by 2H 2026, while Electrification equipment backlog surged to $20B with double-digit EBITDA margins expected in 2025.

Wind remains a drag but losses are narrowing (-42% EBITDA loss improvement in 2024); offshore backlog is being wound down (~$3B remaining) with no new offshore orders anticipated absent substantially better economics, while onshore is profitable at high single-digit margins.
risk_flags: Offshore wind execution risk: ~$1B incremental contract losses in 2024 from blade events and project delays; Vineyard Wind and Dogger Bank still in progress.
North America onshore wind order inflection timing remains uncertain amid interconnection queue congestion and elevated interest rates.
EPC supply chain bottleneck flagged as a potential major constraint to gas turbine project fulfillment timelines, independent of GEV's own delivery schedule.
Inflationary pressures partially offsetting productivity and price gains across all segments.
Capacity ramp execution risk: scaling heavy-duty gas turbine output from 48 to 70-80 units/year by 2H 2026 depends on supplier readiness and lean manufacturing progress.
